title: Let us Learn from Fallen Angels
summary: “For if God spared not the angels that sinned, but cast them down to hell, and delivered them into chains of darkness, to be reserved unto judgment;” (2Peter 2:4). This sermon is about repentance. The Apostolic doctrine of salvation begins with repentance. “Then Peter said unto them, Repent, and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins, and ye shall receive the gift of the Holy Ghost” (Acts 2:38). Fallen angels teach us a powerful lesson about repentance. Firstly, these mighty angels, who were immensely more superior than humans, were sitting in heavenly places. “And he said unto them, I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven” (Luke 10:18). The higher the climb, the greater and more painful the fall! It is when we feel that we are on top of the world, so to speak, that the sin of pride comes and sneaks up on us. Heaven is the ultimate dream for all God’s creatures. Imagine meeting someone who was secure in heaven but was been kicked out – these are the demons that wander in the wilderness of the earth now. There was a time before Lucifer fell that he also walked in righteousness in the Garden of Eden with Adam and Eve, until he became corrupt and corrupted Adam and Eve too.
